zh.Holbrook, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可霍爾布魯克
zh.lifeSummary: Earl Richard Fitzalan於1260年之前出生在英國。他與Mrs Earl Richard Fitzalan結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Holbrook, Suffolk, England: 英国素福爾可霍爾布魯克
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Earl Richard Fitzalan于1260年之前出生在英国。他与Mrs Earl Richard Fitzalan结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Fitzalan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Earl Richard Fitzalan was born before 1260, in England. He married Mrs Earl Richard Fitzalan. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Earl Richard Fitzalan est né avant 1260, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Mrs Earl Richard Fitzalan. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Earl Richard Fitzalan wurde vor 1260 in England geboren. Er hat Mrs Earl Richard Fitzalan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Earl Richard Fitzalan è nato il prima del 1260 nel England. Lui sposato Mrs Earl Richard Fitzalan. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Holbrook, Suffolk, England: イギリススフォルクホールブルーク
ja.lifeSummary: Earl Richard Fitzalanは1260年以前にイギリスで生まれた。Mrs Earl Richard Fitzalanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Earl Richard Fitzalan(은)는 1260년 이전 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 Mrs Earl Richard Fitzalan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Earl Richard Fitzalan nasceu antes 1260, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Mrs Earl Richard Fitzalan. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Earl Richard Fitzalan родился перед 1260 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Earl Richard Fitzalan. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Earl Richard Fitzalan nació antes de 1260, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Mrs Earl Richard Fitzalan. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
